expectations.text: Recommends Python 3.10 specifically, and calls out that the user's existing 3.11 will NOT work (winml-cli wheel pins >=3.10,<3.11).
expectations.evidence: Baseline endorses Python 3.11 / uses py -3.11 -m venv. Doesn't know about the 3.10 pin.

expectations.text: Uses `uv venv --python 3.10` (or an equivalent isolated venv command) rather than installing into system Python or a 3.11 environment.
expectations.evidence: Baseline uses py -3.11 -m venv; uv only mentioned at the end as optional tooling.

expectations.text: Points to the AITK cache as the wheel source today (e.g., `$env:USERPROFILE\.aitk\bin\model_lab_runtime\cache\winml_cli-*.whl`) and acknowledges PyPI is the planned/future path. Does NOT fabricate a generic `pip install winml` URL as if it works today.
expectations.evidence: Baseline interprets 'winml' as Windows ML runtime + onnxruntime-qnn (pip). No mention of AITK cache or winml-cli.

expectations.text: Includes a post-install verification step (e.g., `winml --help` or `winml sys --list-ep`) so the user can confirm the install worked before moving on.
expectations.evidence: Baseline's smoke test verifies onnxruntime-qnn, not the winml CLI.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: Response uses no winml commands (trivially passes).
